94	
95	def _transform_ir(func_body: ir.Block,
96	                  bytecode_version: bc.BytecodeVersion,
97	                  param_constraints: Sequence[tuple[tuple[ir.Var, ...], ParameterConstraint]]):
98	    eliminate_assign_ops(func_body)
99	    dead_code_elimination_pass(func_body)
100	    dataflow_result = dataflow_analysis(func_body, param_constraints)
101	
102	    if not CUDA_TILE_TESTING_DISABLE_DIV:
103	        add_divby_pass(func_body, dataflow_result)
104	
105	    if not CUDA_TILE_TESTING_DISABLE_TOKEN_ORDER:
106	        token_order_pass(func_body, dataflow_result)
107	
108	    rewrite_patterns(func_body)
109	
110	    # Loop invariant code motion needs to run after the token order pass.
111	    # Otherwise, it may incorrectly hoist load operations out of the loop.
112	    hoist_loop_invariants(func_body)
113	
114	    # For version < V_13_3, MakePartitionView must be emitted inline before its consumer.
115	    # Code motion may hoist it to an outer block; copy it back where needed.
116	    if bytecode_version < BytecodeVersion.V_13_3:
117	        unhoist_partition_views(func_body)
118	
119	    split_loops(func_body)
120	    dead_code_elimination_pass(func_body)
121	
122	
123	@dataclass
124	class _KernelParameters:
125	    aggregate_vars: Sequence[ir.Var]
126	    nonconstant_flat_vars: Sequence[tuple[tuple[ir.Var, ...], ParameterConstraint]]
127	
128	
129	def _create_kernel_parameters(parameter_constraints: Sequence[ParameterConstraint],
130	                              parameter_annotations: Sequence[ParameterAnnotationNode],
131	                              parameter_names: Sequence[str],
132	                              parameter_locations: Sequence[Loc],
133	                              ir_ctx: ir.IRContext) -> _KernelParameters:
134	    nonconstant_flat_vars = []
135	    parameter_vars = tuple(ir_ctx.make_var(name, loc)
136	                           for name, loc in zip(parameter_names, parameter_locations, strict=True))
137	
138	    for pos, (constraint, annotation, name, var) in enumerate(
139	            zip(parameter_constraints, parameter_annotations,
140	                parameter_names, parameter_vars, strict=True)):
141	        path = ParameterPath(name, ())
142	        _create_parameter(constraint, annotation, path, var, nonconstant_flat_vars)
143	    return _KernelParameters(parameter_vars, nonconstant_flat_vars)
144	
145	
146	@dataclass
147	class ParameterPath:
148	    name: str
149	    tuple_indices: tuple[int, ...]
150	
151	    def tuple_item(self, index: int) -> "ParameterPath":
152	        return ParameterPath(self.name, self.tuple_indices + (index,))
153	
154	
155	def _create_parameter(
156	        constraint: ParameterConstraint,
157	        annotation: ParameterAnnotationNode,
158	        path: ParameterPath,
159	        var: ir.Var,
160	        nonconstant_flat_vars: list[tuple[tuple[ir.Var, ...], ParameterConstraint]]
161	):
162	    if isinstance(annotation, LeafAnnotationNode):
163	        annotation.validate()
164	
165	        if annotation.array is not None and not isinstance(constraint, ArrayConstraint):
166	            raise _make_constraint_error("ArrayAnnotation/IndexedWithInt64 can only be applied"
167	                                         " to an array or a list-of-array parameter.", path)
168	
169	        if annotation.list is not None and not isinstance(constraint, ListConstraint):
170	            raise _make_constraint_error("ArrayAnnotation/IndexedWithInt64 can only be applied"
171	                                         " to an array or a list-of-array parameter.", path)
172	
173	        if annotation.scalar is not None and not isinstance(constraint, ScalarConstraint):
174	            raise _make_constraint_error(
175	                "ScalarAnnotation/ScalarInt64 can only be applied"
176	                " to a non-constant scalar parameter.", path)
177	
178	    if isinstance(constraint, TupleConstraint):
179	        if isinstance(annotation, LeafAnnotationNode):
180	            item_nodes = [annotation] * len(constraint.items)
181	        elif isinstance(annotation, HomogeneousTupleNode):
182	            item_nodes = [annotation.each] * len(constraint.items)
183	        elif isinstance(annotation, HeterogeneousTupleNode):
184	            if len(annotation.items) != len(constraint.items):
185	                raise _make_constraint_error(
186	                        f"Received a tuple of length {len(constraint.items)}"
187	                        f" but the annotation implies length {len(annotation.items)}.",
188	                        path)
189	            item_nodes = annotation.items
190	        else:
191	            assert False
192	
193	        item_vars = []
194	        for i, (item, node) in enumerate(zip(constraint.items, item_nodes, strict=True)):
195	            item_var = var.ctx.make_var(var.name + f"_{i}", var.loc)
196	            _create_parameter(item, node, path.tuple_item(i), item_var, nonconstant_flat_vars)
197	            item_vars.append(item_var)
198	        build_tuple(item_vars, result_var=var)
199	        return
200	
201	    if not isinstance(annotation, LeafAnnotationNode):
202	        raise _make_constraint_error("Non-tuple parameter is annotated as a tuple.", path)
203	
204	    if annotation.constant and not isinstance(constraint, ConstantConstraint):
205	        raise _make_constraint_error("Expected a scalar/tuple constant,"
206	                                     " as implied by the Constant annotation.", path)
207	
208	    if isinstance(constraint, ConstantConstraint):
209	        if not annotation.constant:
210	            raise _make_constraint_error("ConstantConstraint is only valid for parameters"
211	                                         " annotated as Constant.", path)
212	
213	        loosely_typed_const(constraint.value, result_var=var)
214	        return
215	
216	    if isinstance(constraint, ScalarConstraint):
217	        if annotation.scalar is not None and annotation.scalar.dtype != constraint.dtype:
218	            raise _make_constraint_error(f"ScalarConstraint.dtype {constraint.dtype} does not match"
219	                                         f" the annotated dtype {annotation.scalar.dtype}.", path)
220	        ty = var.ctx.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(constraint.dtype, ())
221	    elif isinstance(constraint, ArrayConstraint):
222	        ty = _get_array_ty(constraint, annotation.array, path, var.ctx.typing_hooks)
223	    elif isinstance(constraint, ListConstraint):
224	        assert isinstance(constraint.element, ArrayConstraint)
225	        array_ann = None if annotation.list is None else annotation.list.element
226	        array_ty = _get_array_ty(constraint.element, array_ann, path, var.ctx.typing_hooks)
227	        ty = ListTy(array_ty)
228	    else:
229	        raise _make_constraint_error(f"Unsupported constraint type"
230	                                     f" '{type(constraint).__name__}'.", path)
231	
232	    var.set_type(ty)
233	    [flat_vars] = flatten_block_parameters([var])
234	    nonconstant_flat_vars.append((flat_vars, constraint))
235	
236	
237	def _make_constraint_error(message: str, path: ParameterPath):
238	    what = f"kernel parameter '{path.name}'"
239	    for tuple_index in path.tuple_indices:
240	        what = f"item #{tuple_index} of {what}"
241	    return TypeError(f"Invalid {what}: {message}")
242	
243	
244	def _resolve_static_shape_axes(array_ann: ArrayAnnotation,
245	                               ndim: int,
246	                               path: ParameterPath) -> list[bool]:
247	    static_shape_mask: list[bool] = [False] * ndim
248	    for axis in array_ann.static_shape_dims:
249	        if not -ndim <= axis < ndim:
250	            raise _make_constraint_error(f"Axis {axis} found in `static_shape_dims`"
251	                                         f" is out of range for an array of rank {ndim}.", path)
252	        normalized = axis + ndim if axis < 0 else axis
253	        if static_shape_mask[normalized]:
254	            raise _make_constraint_error(
255	                    f"Axis {axis} appears more than once in `static_shape_dims`.", path)
256	        static_shape_mask[normalized] = True
257	    return static_shape_mask
258	
259	
260	def _get_array_ty(param: ArrayConstraint,
261	                  array_ann: ArrayAnnotation | None,
262	                  path: ParameterPath,
263	                  typing_hooks: TypingHooks):
264	    if array_ann is None:
265	        array_ann = ArrayAnnotation()
266	
267	    for static_stride, bound in zip(param.stride_constant, param.stride_lower_bound_incl,
268	                                    strict=True):
269	        if static_stride is not None:
270	            continue
271	        if bound is None or bound < 0:
272	            raise _make_constraint_error("Negative strides are currently not supported:"
273	                                         " please specify stride_lower_bound_incl=0", path)
274	
275	    static_shape_mask = _resolve_static_shape_axes(array_ann, param.ndim, path)
276	    array_ty_shape = []
277	    for axis, (annotated_as_static, constraint_size) in enumerate(
278	            zip(static_shape_mask, param.shape_constant, strict=True)):
279	        if annotated_as_static:
280	            if constraint_size is None:
281	                raise _make_constraint_error(
282	                    f"Axis {axis} is annotated as static, but no "
283	                    "constant shape value is available there.", path)
284	            array_ty_shape.append(constraint_size)
285	        else:
286	            array_ty_shape.append(None)
287	
288	    # TODO: `strides` still leaks the dispatcher's stride specialization into the user-visible
289	    # type.
290	    return ArrayTy(param.dtype,
291	                   shape=tuple(array_ty_shape),
292	                   strides=param.stride_constant,
293	                   index_dtype=param.index_dtype,
294	                   typing_hooks=typing_hooks)
295	
296	
297	def _log_mlir(bytecode_buf):
298	    try:
299	        from cuda.tile_internal import _internal_cext
300	    except ImportError:
301	        print("Can't print MLIR because the internal extension is missing. "
302	              "This is currently not a public feature", file=sys.stderr)
303	        return
304	
305	    try:
306	        text = _internal_cext.bytecode_to_mlir_text(bytecode_buf)
307	    except Exception:
308	        print("Failed to print MLIR", file=sys.stderr)
309	        traceback.print_exc()
310	        return
311	
312	    print(f"Lowering\n==== TILEIR MLIR module ====\n\n{text}", file=sys.stderr)
313	
314	
315	def _compiler_crash_dump(final_ir: Sequence[ir.Block],
316	                         func_name: str,
317	                         anonymized_bytecode: bytearray,
318	                         error_msg,
319	                         compiler_flags,
320	                         compiler_version):
321	    debug_info = (
322	        f"error:\n{error_msg}\n\n"
323	        f"compiler flags:\n{compiler_flags}\n\n"
324	        f"compiler version:\n{compiler_version or 'Unkown'}\n\n"
325	        f"cutile version:\n{cutile_version}\n"
326	    )
327	
328	    artifacts = {
329	        f"{func_name}.bytecode": bytes(anonymized_bytecode),
330	        "debug_info.txt": debug_info,
331	    }
332	
333	    for i, func_ir in enumerate(final_ir):
334	        artifacts[f"{func_name}.{i}.cutileir"] = f"{func_ir.body.to_string(include_loc=False)}\n"
335	
336	    timestamp = datetime.datetime.now().timestamp()
337	    zip_filename = os.path.abspath(f"crash_dump_{func_name}_{timestamp}.zip")
338	    print(f"Dumping crash artifacts to {zip_filename}\n", file=sys.stderr)
339	
340	    with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_filename, "w") as z:
341	        for filename, content in artifacts.items():
342	            z.writestr(filename, content)
343	
344	
345	@contextlib.contextmanager
346	def unique_path_from_func_desc(base_dir: str, desc: FunctionDesc, suffix: str, mode: str = "wb"):
347	    prefix = []
348	    if desc.name is not None:
349	        prefix.append(desc.name)
350	    else:
351	        prefix.append("lambda")
352	    prefix.append(Path(desc.filename).stem)
353	    prefix.append(f"ln{desc.line}")
354	    prefix = ".".join(prefix) + "."
355	    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=suffix, prefix=prefix, dir=base_dir,
356	                                     delete=False, mode=mode) as f:
357	        yield f
358	
359	
360	class _TileTypingHooks(TypingHooks):
361	    def get_tensor_like_type(self, dtype: DType, shape: Sequence[int]) -> TileTy:
362	        return TileTy(dtype, shape)
363	
364	
365	class _IrKeeper:
366	    def __init__(self,
367	                 ann_func: AnnotatedFunction,
368	                 func_hir: hir.Function,
369	                 signatures: Sequence[KernelSignature],
370	                 bytecode_version: bc.BytecodeVersion,
371	                 sm_arch: str,
372	                 log_cutile_ir: bool,
373	                 keep_all: bool):
374	        self.ann_func = ann_func
375	        self._func_hir = func_hir
376	        self.signatures = signatures
377	        self.bytecode_version = bytecode_version
378	        self.sm_arch = sm_arch
379	        self._log_cutile_ir = log_cutile_ir
380	        self.final_ir: list[ir.Block | None] | None = [None] * len(signatures) if keep_all else None
381	
382	    @property
383	    def num_signatures(self):
384	        return len(self.signatures)
385	
386	    def get_final_ir(self, signature_index: int) -> ir.Block:
387	        if self.final_ir is None or self.final_ir[signature_index] is None:
388	            sig = self.signatures[signature_index]
389	            param_names = tuple(self.ann_func.pysig.parameters.keys())
390	            ir_ctx = ir.IRContext(log_ir_on_error=self._log_cutile_ir,
391	                                  tileiras_version=self.bytecode_version,
392	                                  typing_hooks=_TileTypingHooks())
393	            with ir.Builder(ir_ctx, self._func_hir.body.loc) as ir_builder:
394	                with tile_impl_registry.as_current():
395	                    params = _create_kernel_parameters(sig.parameters,
396	                                                       self.ann_func.parameter_annotations,
397	                                                       param_names,
398	                                                       self._func_hir.param_locs,
399	                                                       ir_ctx)
400	                    hir2ir(self._func_hir, params.aggregate_vars, ir_ctx)
401	
402	            func_body = ir.Block(ir_ctx, self._func_hir.body.loc)
403	            func_body.params = sum((vars for vars, _ in params.nonconstant_flat_vars), ())
404	            func_body.extend(ir_builder.ops)
405	
406	            _transform_ir(func_body, self.bytecode_version, params.nonconstant_flat_vars)
407	
408	            if self._log_cutile_ir:
409	                code = (f"==== CuTile IR for {self._func_hir.desc.name}==== \n\n"
410	                        f"{func_body.to_string(include_loc=False)}\n\n")
411	                print(f'\n{code}', file=sys.stderr)
412	            check_dtype_support(func_body, self.sm_arch, self.bytecode_version)
413	            if self.final_ir is not None:
414	                self.final_ir[signature_index] = func_body
415	            return func_body
416	        else:
417	            return self.final_ir[signature_index]
418	
419	
420	def _get_bytecode(ir_keeper: _IrKeeper,
421	                  compiler_options: CompilerOptions,
422	                  anonymize_debug_info: bool) -> bytearray:
423	    bytecode_buf = bytearray()
424	
425	    with bc.write_bytecode(num_functions=ir_keeper.num_signatures,
426	                           buf=bytecode_buf, version=ir_keeper.bytecode_version) as writer:
427	        for i in range(ir_keeper.num_signatures):
428	            func_body = ir_keeper.get_final_ir(i)
429	            symbol = ir_keeper.signatures[i].symbol
430	            generate_bytecode_for_kernel(func_body, symbol, compiler_options, ir_keeper.sm_arch,
431	                                         writer, anonymize_debug_attr=anonymize_debug_info)
432	    return bytecode_buf
433	
434	
435	def parse_bytecode_version(version_str: str) -> bc.BytecodeVersion:
436	    for v in _all_bytecode_versions(dev_features_enabled()):
437	        if v.as_string() == version_str:
438	            return v
439	    supported_versions_str = ", ".join(v.as_string() for v in _SUPPORTED_VERSIONS)
440	    raise ValueError(f"Unsupported bytecode version '{version_str}'."
441	                     f" Supported versions are: {supported_versions_str}")
442	
443	
444	@global_compiler_lock
445	def compile_tile(ann_func: AnnotatedFunction | FunctionType,
446	                 signatures: Sequence[KernelSignature],
447	                 sm_arch: str | None = None,
448	                 compiler_options: CompilerOptions = CompilerOptions(),
449	                 context: TileContext = default_tile_context,
450	                 bytecode_version: bc.BytecodeVersion | None = None,
451	                 return_final_ir: bool = False,
452	                 return_bytecode: bool = False,
453	                 return_cubin: bool = True) -> CompilationResult:
454	    if isinstance(ann_func, FunctionType):
455	        ann_func = get_annotated_function(ann_func)
456	    elif not isinstance(ann_func, AnnotatedFunction):
457	        raise TypeError(f"Expected a Python function or an AnnotatedFunction"
458	                        f" for `ann_func`, got {type(ann_func)}")
459	
460	    signatures = list(signatures)
461	    for i in range(len(signatures)):
462	        if signatures[i].symbol is None:
463	            signatures[i] = signatures[i].with_mangled_symbol(ann_func.pyfunc.__name__)
464	
465	    if sm_arch is None:
466	        sm_arch = get_sm_arch()
467	
468	    if bytecode_version is None:
469	        bytecode_version = _get_max_supported_bytecode_version(context.config.temp_dir,
470	                                                               allow_dev=dev_features_enabled())
471	
472	    func_hir = get_function_hir(ann_func.pyfunc, entry_point=True)
473	    func_desc = func_hir.desc
474	    ir_keeper = _IrKeeper(ann_func=ann_func,
475	                          func_hir=func_hir,
476	                          signatures=signatures,
477	                          bytecode_version=bytecode_version,
478	                          sm_arch=sm_arch,
479	                          log_cutile_ir=context.config.log_cutile_ir,
480	                          keep_all=context.config.enable_crash_dump or return_final_ir)
481	
482	    need_bytecode = return_bytecode or return_cubin
483	    if not need_bytecode:
484	        for i in range(ir_keeper.num_signatures):
485	            ir_keeper.get_final_ir(i)
486	        return CompilationResult(signatures, final_ir=ir_keeper.final_ir)
487	
488	    bytecode_buf = _get_bytecode(ir_keeper, compiler_options, anonymize_debug_info=False)
489	
490	    if context.config.log_tileir:
491	        _log_mlir(bytecode_buf)
492	
493	    if CUDA_TILE_DUMP_BYTECODE is not None:
494	        if not os.path.isdir(CUDA_TILE_DUMP_BYTECODE):
495	            os.makedirs(CUDA_TILE_DUMP_BYTECODE)
496	        with unique_path_from_func_desc(CUDA_TILE_DUMP_BYTECODE,
497	                                        func_desc, '.tileirbc') as f:
498	            print(f"Dumping TILEIR bytecode to file: {f.name}", file=sys.stderr)
499	            f.write(bytecode_buf)
500	
501	    # Write MLIR module to file
502	    if CUDA_TILE_DUMP_TILEIR is not None:
503	        try:
504	            from cuda.tile_internal._internal_cext import bytecode_to_mlir_text
505	            mlir_text = bytecode_to_mlir_text(bytecode_buf)
506	            if not os.path.isdir(CUDA_TILE_DUMP_TILEIR):
507	                os.makedirs(CUDA_TILE_DUMP_TILEIR)
508	            with unique_path_from_func_desc(CUDA_TILE_DUMP_TILEIR,
509	                                            func_desc, '.tileir', mode="w") as f:
510	                print(f"Dumping TILEIR MLIR module to file: {f.name}", file=sys.stderr)
511	                f.write(mlir_text)
512	        except ImportError:
513	            print("Can't print MLIR because the internal extension is missing. "
514	                  "This is currently not a public feature.", file=sys.stderr)
515	
516	    ret = CompilationResult(signatures,
517	                            bytecode=bytecode_buf if return_bytecode else None,
518	                            final_ir=ir_keeper.final_ir)
519	    if not return_cubin:
520	        return ret
521	
522	    # Check disk cache before invoking tileiras
523	    cache_dir = context.config.cache_dir
524	    compiler_ver = _get_compiler_version_string()
525	    key = None
526	    if cache_dir is None:
527	        logger.debug("disk cache disabled: context.config.cache_dir is not set")
528	    elif compiler_ver is None:
529	        logger.warning("disk cache disabled: compiler version is unknown")
530	    else:
531	        effective_opt, device_debug = _tileiras_effective_opt_and_device_debug(
532	            compiler_options, sm_arch)
533	        key = cache_key(
534	            compiler_ver, sm_arch, effective_opt, bytecode_buf, device_debug
535	        )
536	        cubin = cache_lookup(cache_dir, key)
537	        if cubin is not None:
538	            ret.cubin = cubin
539	            return ret
540	
541	    # Compile MLIR module and generate cubin
542	    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.bytecode', prefix=func_desc.name,
543	                                     dir=context.config.temp_dir, delete=False) as f:
544	        f.write(bytecode_buf)
545	        f.flush()
546	
547	        try:
548	            cubin_file = compile_cubin(f.name, compiler_options, sm_arch,
549	                                       timeout_sec=context.config.compiler_timeout_sec)
550	        except TileCompilerError as e:
551	            if context.config.enable_crash_dump:
552	                anonymized_bytecode = _get_bytecode(ir_keeper, compiler_options,
553	                                                    anonymize_debug_info=True)
554	
555	                _compiler_crash_dump(ir_keeper.final_ir, func_desc.name,
556	                                     anonymized_bytecode, e.message,
557	                                     e.compiler_flags, e.compiler_version)
558	
559	            raise e
560	        ret.cubin = Path(cubin_file).read_bytes()
561	
562	    if cache_dir is not None and key is not None:
563	        cache_store(cache_dir, key, ret.cubin)
564	        evict_lru(cache_dir, context.config.cache_size_limit)
565	
566	    return ret
567	
568	
569	def _tileiras_effective_opt_and_device_debug(
570	    compiler_options: CompilerOptions, sm_arch: str
571	) -> tuple[int, bool]:
572	    """
573	    When EXPERIMENTAL_CUDA_TILE_DEBUG_BUILD is set, tileiras must use -O0 and
574	    --device-debug; the disk cache key must match (see cache_key).
575	    """
576	    if EXPERIMENTAL_CUDA_TILE_DEBUG_BUILD:
577	        return 0, True
578	    return compiler_options.opt_level_for_target(sm_arch), False
579	
580	
581	def is_windows() -> bool:
582	    return sys.platform == "win32"
583	
584	
585	def _get_cuda_home() -> Optional[str]:
586	    if is_windows():
587	        if (ret := os.environ.get("CUDA_PATH")):
588	            return ret
589	    return os.environ.get("CUDA_HOME")
590	
591	
592	@dataclass
593	class _CompilerBinary:
594	    path: str
595	    bin_path: str
596	    ld_path: str
597	    pass_cuda_home_var: bool
598	
599	    def run(self,
600	            args: list[str],
601	            flags: list[str],
602	            timeout_sec: float | None = None):
603	        command = [self.path, *args]
604	
605	        logger.debug(f"Invoke tile compiler: {' '.join(command + flags)}\n"
606	                     f"LD_LIBRARY_PATH:{self.ld_path}\n"
607	                     f"PATH:{self.bin_path}")
608	        try:
609	            env = os.environ.copy()
610	            env['LD_LIBRARY_PATH'] = self.ld_path
611	            env['PATH'] = self.bin_path
612	            if not self.pass_cuda_home_var:
613	                for key in {"CUDA_HOME", "CUDA_PATH"}:
614	                    env.pop(key, None)
615	            subprocess.run(command + flags, env=env, check=True, capture_output=True,
616	                           timeout=timeout_sec)
617	        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
618	            raise TileCompilerExecutionError(e.returncode, e.stderr.decode(), ' '.join(flags),
619	                                             _try_get_compiler_version(self.path))
620	        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
621	            message = (f"`tileiras` compiler exceeded timeout {timeout_sec}s. "
622	                       "Using a smaller tile size may reduce compilation time.")
623	            raise TileCompilerTimeoutError(message, ' '.join(flags),
624	                                           _try_get_compiler_version(self.path))
625	
626	
627	_PIP_TILEIRAS_PACKAGES = (
628	    "nvidia-cuda-tileiras",
629	    "nvidia-cuda-nvcc",
630	    "nvidia-nvvm",
631	)
632	
633	
634	def _get_major_minor(version_str: str) -> tuple[int, int]:
635	    parts = version_str.split(".")
636	    return int(parts[0]), int(parts[1])
637	
638	
639	def _find_pip_tileiras() -> Optional[str]:
640	    versions: dict[str, str] = {}
641	    for pkg in _PIP_TILEIRAS_PACKAGES:
642	        try:
643	            versions[pkg] = importlib.metadata.version(pkg)
644	        except importlib.metadata.PackageNotFoundError:
645	            return None
646	
647	    majors_minors = {pkg: _get_major_minor(v) for pkg, v in versions.items()}
648	    unique = set(majors_minors.values())
649	    if len(unique) != 1:
650	        details = ", ".join(f"{pkg} {versions[pkg]}" for pkg in _PIP_TILEIRAS_PACKAGES)
651	        warnings.warn(
652	            f"Installed NVIDIA pip packages have mismatched versions ({details}). "
653	            "Falling back to system tileiras.",
654	            stacklevel=3,
655	        )
656	        return None
657	
658	    try:
659	        import nvidia.cu13 as cu13_pkg
660	        cu13_root = cu13_pkg.__path__[0]
661	    except (ImportError, AttributeError, IndexError):
662	        logger.debug("Fail to get nvidia.cu13 package path.", exc_info=True)
663	        return None
664	
665	    pip_bin_dir = os.path.join(cu13_root, "bin")
666	    res = shutil.which("tileiras", path=pip_bin_dir)
667	    if res is None:
668	        logger.debug("Fail to find tileiras under nvidia.cu13 path.")
669	        return None
670	
671	    logger.debug(f"Found tileiras from pip package: {res}")
672	    return res
673	
674	
675	@cache
676	def _find_compiler_bin() -> _CompilerBinary:
677	    bin_path = os.environ.get('PATH', '')
678	    ld_path = os.environ.get('LD_LIBRARY_PATH', "") if not is_windows() else ""
679	
680	    # search from nvidia-cuda-tileiras pip package
681	    logger.debug("Searching tileiras from nvidia pip package")
682	    res = _find_pip_tileiras()
683	    if res is not None:
684	        return _CompilerBinary(res, bin_path, ld_path, pass_cuda_home_var=False)
685	
686	    # search under PATH
687	    logger.debug(f"Searching tileiras: {bin_path}")
688	    if (res := shutil.which("tileiras")):
689	        return _CompilerBinary(res, bin_path, ld_path, pass_cuda_home_var=True)
690	
691	    # search under CUDA_HOME
692	    if (cuda_home := _get_cuda_home()):
693	        cuda_bin_path = os.path.join(cuda_home, 'bin')
694	        logger.debug(f"Searching tileiras: {cuda_bin_path}")
695	        if (res := shutil.which("tileiras", path=cuda_bin_path)):
696	            bin_path = bin_path + ":" + cuda_bin_path
697	            return _CompilerBinary(res, bin_path, ld_path, pass_cuda_home_var=True)
698	
699	    # Try default CUDA Toolkit installation paths as a fallback
700	    res = _find_compiler_in_default_cuda_toolkit_paths()
701	    if res is not None:
702	        tileiras_path, bin_path = res
703	        return _CompilerBinary(tileiras_path, bin_path, ld_path, pass_cuda_home_var=False)
704	
705	    cuda_home_var = "CUDA_PATH" if is_windows() else "CUDA_HOME"
706	    raise FileNotFoundError("'tileiras' compiler not found, "
707	                            "make sure it is available as a python package via "
708	                            "`pip install cuda-tile[tileiras]` or "
709	                            f"available in $PATH or ${cuda_home_var}/bin via system CTK (13.1+)"
710	                            " installation.")
711	
712	
713	_SUPPORTED_VERSIONS = [
714	    BytecodeVersion.V_13_1,
715	    BytecodeVersion.V_13_2,
716	    BytecodeVersion.V_13_3,
717	]
718	
719	
720	def _all_bytecode_versions(allow_dev: bool = False) -> Sequence[BytecodeVersion]:
721	    return BytecodeVersion if allow_dev else _SUPPORTED_VERSIONS
722	
723	
724	@cache
725	def _get_max_supported_bytecode_version(temp_dir: str, allow_dev: bool = False) -> BytecodeVersion:
726	    binary = _find_compiler_bin()
727	    flags = ["--gpu-name", "sm_120"]
728	    for version in reversed(_all_bytecode_versions(allow_dev)):
729	        probe = bytearray()
730	        with bc.write_bytecode(num_functions=0, buf=probe, version=version):
731	            pass
732	
733	        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.bytecode', prefix=f"probe{version}",
734	                                         dir=temp_dir, delete=False) as f_in, \
735	            tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.cubin', prefix=f"probe{version}",
736	                                        dir=temp_dir, delete=False) as f_out:
737	            f_in.write(probe)
738	
739	        try:
740	            binary.run([f_in.name, "-o", f_out.name], flags)
741	        except TileCompilerError:
742	            continue
743	
744	        return version
745	
746	    warnings.warn("Failed to detect the maximum supported TileIR bytecode version;"
747	                  " falling back to 13.1.")
748	    return BytecodeVersion.V_13_1
749	
750	
751	def _find_compiler_in_default_cuda_toolkit_paths() -> tuple[str, str] | None:
752	    binary_name = "tileiras.exe" if is_windows() else "tileiras"
753	    for toolkit_path in _get_default_cuda_toolkit_paths():
754	        bin_path = os.path.join(toolkit_path, "bin")
755	        p = os.path.join(bin_path, binary_name)
756	        if os.path.exists(p) and os.access(p, os.X_OK) and not os.path.isdir(p):
757	            return p, bin_path
758	    return None
759	
760	
761	def _get_default_cuda_toolkit_paths() -> list[str]:
762	    candidates = []
763	
764	    if os.name == "nt":
765	        prefix = "C:\\Program Files\\NVIDIA GPU Computing Toolkit\\CUDA"
766	        regex = re.compile(r"[vV]([0-9]+)(\.[0-9]+)?")
767	    else:
768	        prefix = "/usr/local"
769	        regex = re.compile(r"cuda-([0-9]+)(\.[0-9]+)?")
770	        candidates.append((math.inf, math.inf, "cuda"))
771	
772	    for subdir in os.listdir(prefix):
773	        m = re.fullmatch(regex, subdir)
774	        if m is None:
775	            continue
776	        major = int(m.group(1))
777	        minor = m.group(2)
778	        minor = math.inf if minor is None else int(minor[1:])
779	        candidates.append((major, minor, subdir))
780	
781	    return [os.path.join(prefix, subdir)
782	            for _, _, subdir in reversed(sorted(candidates))]
783	
784	
785	def _try_get_compiler_version(compiler_bin) -> Optional[str]:
786	    try:
787	        res = subprocess.run([str(compiler_bin), "--version"],
788	                             check=True, capture_output=True, text=True)
789	        return res.stdout
790	    except Exception:
791	        return None
792	
793	
794	@cache
795	def _get_compiler_version_string() -> str | None:
796	    binary = _find_compiler_bin()
797	    version = _try_get_compiler_version(binary.path)
798	    return version
799	
800	
801	@cache
802	def get_sm_arch() -> str:
803	    major, minor = get_compute_capability()
804	    return f'sm_{major}{minor}'
805	
806	
807	def compile_cubin(
808	        fname_bytecode: str,
809	        compiler_options: CompilerOptions,
810	        sm_arch: str,
811	        timeout_sec: Optional[float]) -> Path:
812	    binary = _find_compiler_bin()
813	    fname_cubin = Path(fname_bytecode).with_suffix(".cubin")
814	    effective_opt, use_device_debug = _tileiras_effective_opt_and_device_debug(
815	        compiler_options, sm_arch
816	    )
817	
818	    args = [str(fname_bytecode), "-o", str(fname_cubin)]
819	
820	    flags: list[str] = [
821	        "--gpu-name",
822	        sm_arch,
823	        f"-O{effective_opt}",
824	    ]
825	    if use_device_debug:
826	        flags.append("--device-debug")
827	    else:
828	        flags.append("--lineinfo")
829	
830	    binary.run(args, flags, timeout_sec)
831	    return fname_cubin
832

# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) <2025> NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
#
# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
import importlib.metadata
import math
import re
import warnings
import contextlib
from dataclasses import dataclass
import datetime
import functools
from functools import cache
import logging
import os
from pathlib import Path
import subprocess
import shutil
import sys
import tempfile
import threading
import traceback
from types import FunctionType
from typing import Optional, Sequence
import zipfile

from cuda.tile import ArrayAnnotation
from cuda.tile._annotated_function import (
    AnnotatedFunction, HeterogeneousTupleNode, HomogeneousTupleNode, LeafAnnotationNode,
    ParameterAnnotationNode, get_annotated_function)
from cuda.tile._bytecode.version import BytecodeVersion
from cuda.tile._cext import get_compute_capability, TileContext, default_tile_context
from cuda.tile._compiler_options import CompilerOptions
from cuda.tile._datatype import DType
from cuda.tile._exception import (
    TileCompilerError,
    TileCompilerExecutionError,
    TileCompilerTimeoutError, FunctionDesc, Loc
)
from cuda.tile._ir import ir, hir
from cuda.tile._ir.ir import TypingHooks
from cuda.tile._ir.aggregate_support import flatten_block_parameters
from cuda.tile._ir.ops import loosely_typed_const, tile_impl_registry, build_tuple
from cuda.tile._ir.type import TileTy, ArrayTy, ListTy
from cuda.tile._passes.ast2hir import get_function_hir
from cuda.tile._passes.code_motion import hoist_loop_invariants
from cuda.tile._passes.unhoist_partition_views import unhoist_partition_views
from cuda.tile._passes.eliminate_assign_ops import eliminate_assign_ops
from cuda.tile._passes.hir2ir import hir2ir
from cuda.tile._passes.loop_split import split_loops
from cuda.tile._passes.rewrite_patterns import rewrite_patterns
from cuda.tile._cext import dev_features_enabled
from cuda.tile._debug import (
    CUDA_TILE_TESTING_DISABLE_DIV,
    CUDA_TILE_TESTING_DISABLE_TOKEN_ORDER,
    CUDA_TILE_DUMP_BYTECODE,
    CUDA_TILE_DUMP_TILEIR,
    EXPERIMENTAL_CUDA_TILE_DEBUG_BUILD,
)

from cuda.tile._passes.dataflow_analysis import dataflow_analysis
from cuda.tile._passes.check_dtype_support import check_dtype_support
from cuda.tile._passes.dce import dead_code_elimination_pass
from cuda.tile._passes.propagate_divby import add_divby_pass
from cuda.tile._passes.token_order import token_order_pass
from cuda.tile._cache import cache_key, cache_lookup, cache_store, evict_lru
from cuda.tile._ir2bytecode import generate_bytecode_for_kernel
from cuda.tile._version import __version__ as cutile_version
import cuda.tile._bytecode as bc
from cuda.tile.compilation._signature import KernelSignature, ParameterConstraint, \
    ScalarConstraint, ArrayConstraint, ListConstraint, TupleConstraint, ConstantConstraint

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)


@dataclass
class CompilationResult:
    kernel_signatures: Sequence[KernelSignature]
    cubin: bytes | None = None
    bytecode: bytearray | None = None
    final_ir: Sequence[ir.Block] | None = None


# Create a global lock
_compiler_lock = threading.RLock()


def global_compiler_lock(func):
    @functools.wraps(func)
    def wrapper(*args, **kwargs):
        with _compiler_lock:
            return func(*args, **kwargs)
    return wrapper


def _transform_ir(func_body: ir.Block,
                  bytecode_version: bc.BytecodeVersion,
                  param_constraints: Sequence[tuple[tuple[ir.Var, ...], ParameterConstraint]]):
    eliminate_assign_ops(func_body)
    dead_code_elimination_pass(func_body)
    dataflow_result = dataflow_analysis(func_body, param_constraints)

    if not CUDA_TILE_TESTING_DISABLE_DIV:
        add_divby_pass(func_body, dataflow_result)

    if not CUDA_TILE_TESTING_DISABLE_TOKEN_ORDER:
        token_order_pass(func_body, dataflow_result)

    rewrite_patterns(func_body)

    # Loop invariant code motion needs to run after the token order pass.
    # Otherwise, it may incorrectly hoist load operations out of the loop.
    hoist_loop_invariants(func_body)

    # For version < V_13_3, MakePartitionView must be emitted inline before its consumer.
    # Code motion may hoist it to an outer block; copy it back where needed.
    if bytecode_version < BytecodeVersion.V_13_3:
        unhoist_partition_views(func_body)

    split_loops(func_body)
    dead_code_elimination_pass(func_body)


@dataclass
class _KernelParameters:
    aggregate_vars: Sequence[ir.Var]
    nonconstant_flat_vars: Sequence[tuple[tuple[ir.Var, ...], ParameterConstraint]]


def _create_kernel_parameters(parameter_constraints: Sequence[ParameterConstraint],
                              parameter_annotations: Sequence[ParameterAnnotationNode],
                              parameter_names: Sequence[str],
                              parameter_locations: Sequence[Loc],
                              ir_ctx: ir.IRContext) -> _KernelParameters:
    nonconstant_flat_vars = []
    parameter_vars = tuple(ir_ctx.make_var(name, loc)
                           for name, loc in zip(parameter_names, parameter_locations, strict=True))

    for pos, (constraint, annotation, name, var) in enumerate(
            zip(parameter_constraints, parameter_annotations,
                parameter_names, parameter_vars, strict=True)):
        path = ParameterPath(name, ())
        _create_parameter(constraint, annotation, path, var, nonconstant_flat_vars)
    return _KernelParameters(parameter_vars, nonconstant_flat_vars)


@dataclass
class ParameterPath:
    name: str
    tuple_indices: tuple[int, ...]

    def tuple_item(self, index: int) -> "ParameterPath":
        return ParameterPath(self.name, self.tuple_indices + (index,))


def _create_parameter(
        constraint: ParameterConstraint,
        annotation: ParameterAnnotationNode,
        path: ParameterPath,
        var: ir.Var,
        nonconstant_flat_vars: list[tuple[tuple[ir.Var, ...], ParameterConstraint]]
):
    if isinstance(annotation, LeafAnnotationNode):
        annotation.validate()

        if annotation.array is not None and not isinstance(constraint, ArrayConstraint):
            raise _make_constraint_error("ArrayAnnotation/IndexedWithInt64 can only be applied"
                                         " to an array or a list-of-array parameter.", path)

        if annotation.list is not None and not isinstance(constraint, ListConstraint):
            raise _make_constraint_error("ArrayAnnotation/IndexedWithInt64 can only be applied"
                                         " to an array or a list-of-array parameter.", path)

        if annotation.scalar is not None and not isinstance(constraint, ScalarConstraint):
            raise _make_constraint_error(
                "ScalarAnnotation/ScalarInt64 can only be applied"
                " to a non-constant scalar parameter.", path)

    if isinstance(constraint, TupleConstraint):
        if isinstance(annotation, LeafAnnotationNode):
            item_nodes = [annotation] * len(constraint.items)
        elif isinstance(annotation, HomogeneousTupleNode):
            item_nodes = [annotation.each] * len(constraint.items)
        elif isinstance(annotation, HeterogeneousTupleNode):
            if len(annotation.items) != len(constraint.items):
                raise _make_constraint_error(
                        f"Received a tuple of length {len(constraint.items)}"
                        f" but the annotation implies length {len(annotation.items)}.",
                        path)
            item_nodes = annotation.items
        else:
            assert False

        item_vars = []
        for i, (item, node) in enumerate(zip(constraint.items, item_nodes, strict=True)):
            item_var = var.ctx.make_var(var.name + f"_{i}", var.loc)
            _create_parameter(item, node, path.tuple_item(i), item_var, nonconstant_flat_vars)
            item_vars.append(item_var)
        build_tuple(item_vars, result_var=var)
        return

    if not isinstance(annotation, LeafAnnotationNode):
        raise _make_constraint_error("Non-tuple parameter is annotated as a tuple.", path)

    if annotation.constant and not isinstance(constraint, ConstantConstraint):
        raise _make_constraint_error("Expected a scalar/tuple constant,"
                                     " as implied by the Constant annotation.", path)

    if isinstance(constraint, ConstantConstraint):
        if not annotation.constant:
            raise _make_constraint_error("ConstantConstraint is only valid for parameters"
                                         " annotated as Constant.", path)

        loosely_typed_const(constraint.value, result_var=var)
        return

    if isinstance(constraint, ScalarConstraint):
        if annotation.scalar is not None and annotation.scalar.dtype != constraint.dtype:
            raise _make_constraint_error(f"ScalarConstraint.dtype {constraint.dtype} does not match"
                                         f" the annotated dtype {annotation.scalar.dtype}.", path)
        ty = var.ctx.typing_hooks.get_tensor_like_type(constraint.dtype, ())
    elif isinstance(constraint, ArrayConstraint):
        ty = _get_array_ty(constraint, annotation.array, path, var.ctx.typing_hooks)
    elif isinstance(constraint, ListConstraint):
        assert isinstance(constraint.element, ArrayConstraint)
        array_ann = None if annotation.list is None else annotation.list.element
        array_ty = _get_array_ty(constraint.element, array_ann, path, var.ctx.typing_hooks)
        ty = ListTy(array_ty)
    else:
        raise _make_constraint_error(f"Unsupported constraint type"
                                     f" '{type(constraint).__name__}'.", path)

    var.set_type(ty)
    [flat_vars] = flatten_block_parameters([var])
    nonconstant_flat_vars.append((flat_vars, constraint))


def _make_constraint_error(message: str, path: ParameterPath):
    what = f"kernel parameter '{path.name}'"
    for tuple_index in path.tuple_indices:
        what = f"item #{tuple_index} of {what}"
    return TypeError(f"Invalid {what}: {message}")


def _resolve_static_shape_axes(array_ann: ArrayAnnotation,
                               ndim: int,
                               path: ParameterPath) -> list[bool]:
    static_shape_mask: list[bool] = [False] * ndim
    for axis in array_ann.static_shape_dims:
        if not -ndim <= axis < ndim:
            raise _make_constraint_error(f"Axis {axis} found in `static_shape_dims`"
                                         f" is out of range for an array of rank {ndim}.", path)
        normalized = axis + ndim if axis < 0 else axis
        if static_shape_mask[normalized]:
            raise _make_constraint_error(
                    f"Axis {axis} appears more than once in `static_shape_dims`.", path)
        static_shape_mask[normalized] = True
    return static_shape_mask


def _get_array_ty(param: ArrayConstraint,
                  array_ann: ArrayAnnotation | None,
                  path: ParameterPath,
                  typing_hooks: TypingHooks):
    if array_ann is None:
        array_ann = ArrayAnnotation()

    for static_stride, bound in zip(param.stride_constant, param.stride_lower_bound_incl,
                                    strict=True):
        if static_stride is not None:
            continue
        if bound is None or bound < 0:
            raise _make_constraint_error("Negative strides are currently not supported:"
                                         " please specify stride_lower_bound_incl=0", path)

    static_shape_mask = _resolve_static_shape_axes(array_ann, param.ndim, path)
    array_ty_shape = []
    for axis, (annotated_as_static, constraint_size) in enumerate(
            zip(static_shape_mask, param.shape_constant, strict=True)):
        if annotated_as_static:
            if constraint_size is None:
                raise _make_constraint_error(
                    f"Axis {axis} is annotated as static, but no "
                    "constant shape value is available there.", path)
            array_ty_shape.append(constraint_size)
        else:
            array_ty_shape.append(None)

    # TODO: `strides` still leaks the dispatcher's stride specialization into the user-visible
    # type.
    return ArrayTy(param.dtype,
                   shape=tuple(array_ty_shape),
                   strides=param.stride_constant,
                   index_dtype=param.index_dtype,
                   typing_hooks=typing_hooks)


def _log_mlir(bytecode_buf):
    try:
        from cuda.tile_internal import _internal_cext
    except ImportError:
        print("Can't print MLIR because the internal extension is missing. "
              "This is currently not a public feature", file=sys.stderr)
        return

    try:
        text = _internal_cext.bytecode_to_mlir_text(bytecode_buf)
    except Exception:
        print("Failed to print MLIR", file=sys.stderr)
        traceback.print_exc()
        return

    print(f"Lowering\n==== TILEIR MLIR module ====\n\n{text}", file=sys.stderr)


def _compiler_crash_dump(final_ir: Sequence[ir.Block],
                         func_name: str,
                         anonymized_bytecode: bytearray,
                         error_msg,
                         compiler_flags,
                         compiler_version):
    debug_info = (
        f"error:\n{error_msg}\n\n"
        f"compiler flags:\n{compiler_flags}\n\n"
        f"compiler version:\n{compiler_version or 'Unkown'}\n\n"
        f"cutile version:\n{cutile_version}\n"
    )

    artifacts = {
        f"{func_name}.bytecode": bytes(anonymized_bytecode),
        "debug_info.txt": debug_info,
    }

    for i, func_ir in enumerate(final_ir):
        artifacts[f"{func_name}.{i}.cutileir"] = f"{func_ir.body.to_string(include_loc=False)}\n"

    timestamp = datetime.datetime.now().timestamp()
    zip_filename = os.path.abspath(f"crash_dump_{func_name}_{timestamp}.zip")
    print(f"Dumping crash artifacts to {zip_filename}\n", file=sys.stderr)

    with zipfile.ZipFile(zip_filename, "w") as z:
        for filename, content in artifacts.items():
            z.writestr(filename, content)


@contextlib.contextmanager
def unique_path_from_func_desc(base_dir: str, desc: FunctionDesc, suffix: str, mode: str = "wb"):
    prefix = []
    if desc.name is not None:
        prefix.append(desc.name)
    else:
        prefix.append("lambda")
    prefix.append(Path(desc.filename).stem)
    prefix.append(f"ln{desc.line}")
    prefix = ".".join(prefix) + "."
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix=suffix, prefix=prefix, dir=base_dir,
                                     delete=False, mode=mode) as f:
        yield f


class _TileTypingHooks(TypingHooks):
    def get_tensor_like_type(self, dtype: DType, shape: Sequence[int]) -> TileTy:
        return TileTy(dtype, shape)


class _IrKeeper:
    def __init__(self,
                 ann_func: AnnotatedFunction,
                 func_hir: hir.Function,
                 signatures: Sequence[KernelSignature],
                 bytecode_version: bc.BytecodeVersion,
                 sm_arch: str,
                 log_cutile_ir: bool,
                 keep_all: bool):
        self.ann_func = ann_func
        self._func_hir = func_hir
        self.signatures = signatures
        self.bytecode_version = bytecode_version
        self.sm_arch = sm_arch
        self._log_cutile_ir = log_cutile_ir
        self.final_ir: list[ir.Block | None] | None = [None] * len(signatures) if keep_all else None

    @property
    def num_signatures(self):
        return len(self.signatures)

    def get_final_ir(self, signature_index: int) -> ir.Block:
        if self.final_ir is None or self.final_ir[signature_index] is None:
            sig = self.signatures[signature_index]
            param_names = tuple(self.ann_func.pysig.parameters.keys())
            ir_ctx = ir.IRContext(log_ir_on_error=self._log_cutile_ir,
                                  tileiras_version=self.bytecode_version,
                                  typing_hooks=_TileTypingHooks())
            with ir.Builder(ir_ctx, self._func_hir.body.loc) as ir_builder:
                with tile_impl_registry.as_current():
                    params = _create_kernel_parameters(sig.parameters,
                                                       self.ann_func.parameter_annotations,
                                                       param_names,
                                                       self._func_hir.param_locs,
                                                       ir_ctx)
                    hir2ir(self._func_hir, params.aggregate_vars, ir_ctx)

            func_body = ir.Block(ir_ctx, self._func_hir.body.loc)
            func_body.params = sum((vars for vars, _ in params.nonconstant_flat_vars), ())
            func_body.extend(ir_builder.ops)

            _transform_ir(func_body, self.bytecode_version, params.nonconstant_flat_vars)

            if self._log_cutile_ir:
                code = (f"==== CuTile IR for {self._func_hir.desc.name}==== \n\n"
                        f"{func_body.to_string(include_loc=False)}\n\n")
                print(f'\n{code}', file=sys.stderr)
            check_dtype_support(func_body, self.sm_arch, self.bytecode_version)
            if self.final_ir is not None:
                self.final_ir[signature_index] = func_body
            return func_body
        else:
            return self.final_ir[signature_index]


def _get_bytecode(ir_keeper: _IrKeeper,
                  compiler_options: CompilerOptions,
                  anonymize_debug_info: bool) -> bytearray:
    bytecode_buf = bytearray()

    with bc.write_bytecode(num_functions=ir_keeper.num_signatures,
                           buf=bytecode_buf, version=ir_keeper.bytecode_version) as writer:
        for i in range(ir_keeper.num_signatures):
            func_body = ir_keeper.get_final_ir(i)
            symbol = ir_keeper.signatures[i].symbol
            generate_bytecode_for_kernel(func_body, symbol, compiler_options, ir_keeper.sm_arch,
                                         writer, anonymize_debug_attr=anonymize_debug_info)
    return bytecode_buf


def parse_bytecode_version(version_str: str) -> bc.BytecodeVersion:
    for v in _all_bytecode_versions(dev_features_enabled()):
        if v.as_string() == version_str:
            return v
    supported_versions_str = ", ".join(v.as_string() for v in _SUPPORTED_VERSIONS)
    raise ValueError(f"Unsupported bytecode version '{version_str}'."
                     f" Supported versions are: {supported_versions_str}")


@global_compiler_lock
def compile_tile(ann_func: AnnotatedFunction | FunctionType,
                 signatures: Sequence[KernelSignature],
                 sm_arch: str | None = None,
                 compiler_options: CompilerOptions = CompilerOptions(),
                 context: TileContext = default_tile_context,
                 bytecode_version: bc.BytecodeVersion | None = None,
                 return_final_ir: bool = False,
                 return_bytecode: bool = False,
                 return_cubin: bool = True) -> CompilationResult:
    if isinstance(ann_func, FunctionType):
        ann_func = get_annotated_function(ann_func)
    elif not isinstance(ann_func, AnnotatedFunction):
        raise TypeError(f"Expected a Python function or an AnnotatedFunction"
                        f" for `ann_func`, got {type(ann_func)}")

    signatures = list(signatures)
    for i in range(len(signatures)):
        if signatures[i].symbol is None:
            signatures[i] = signatures[i].with_mangled_symbol(ann_func.pyfunc.__name__)

    if sm_arch is None:
        sm_arch = get_sm_arch()

    if bytecode_version is None:
        bytecode_version = _get_max_supported_bytecode_version(context.config.temp_dir,
                                                               allow_dev=dev_features_enabled())

    func_hir = get_function_hir(ann_func.pyfunc, entry_point=True)
    func_desc = func_hir.desc
    ir_keeper = _IrKeeper(ann_func=ann_func,
                          func_hir=func_hir,
                          signatures=signatures,
                          bytecode_version=bytecode_version,
                          sm_arch=sm_arch,
                          log_cutile_ir=context.config.log_cutile_ir,
                          keep_all=context.config.enable_crash_dump or return_final_ir)

    need_bytecode = return_bytecode or return_cubin
    if not need_bytecode:
        for i in range(ir_keeper.num_signatures):
            ir_keeper.get_final_ir(i)
        return CompilationResult(signatures, final_ir=ir_keeper.final_ir)

    bytecode_buf = _get_bytecode(ir_keeper, compiler_options, anonymize_debug_info=False)

    if context.config.log_tileir:
        _log_mlir(bytecode_buf)

    if CUDA_TILE_DUMP_BYTECODE is not None:
        if not os.path.isdir(CUDA_TILE_DUMP_BYTECODE):
            os.makedirs(CUDA_TILE_DUMP_BYTECODE)
        with unique_path_from_func_desc(CUDA_TILE_DUMP_BYTECODE,
                                        func_desc, '.tileirbc') as f:
            print(f"Dumping TILEIR bytecode to file: {f.name}", file=sys.stderr)
            f.write(bytecode_buf)

    # Write MLIR module to file
    if CUDA_TILE_DUMP_TILEIR is not None:
        try:
            from cuda.tile_internal._internal_cext import bytecode_to_mlir_text
            mlir_text = bytecode_to_mlir_text(bytecode_buf)
            if not os.path.isdir(CUDA_TILE_DUMP_TILEIR):
                os.makedirs(CUDA_TILE_DUMP_TILEIR)
            with unique_path_from_func_desc(CUDA_TILE_DUMP_TILEIR,
                                            func_desc, '.tileir', mode="w") as f:
                print(f"Dumping TILEIR MLIR module to file: {f.name}", file=sys.stderr)
                f.write(mlir_text)
        except ImportError:
            print("Can't print MLIR because the internal extension is missing. "
                  "This is currently not a public feature.", file=sys.stderr)

    ret = CompilationResult(signatures,
                            bytecode=bytecode_buf if return_bytecode else None,
                            final_ir=ir_keeper.final_ir)
    if not return_cubin:
        return ret

    # Check disk cache before invoking tileiras
    cache_dir = context.config.cache_dir
    compiler_ver = _get_compiler_version_string()
    key = None
    if cache_dir is None:
        logger.debug("disk cache disabled: context.config.cache_dir is not set")
    elif compiler_ver is None:
        logger.warning("disk cache disabled: compiler version is unknown")
    else:
        effective_opt, device_debug = _tileiras_effective_opt_and_device_debug(
            compiler_options, sm_arch)
        key = cache_key(
            compiler_ver, sm_arch, effective_opt, bytecode_buf, device_debug
        )
        cubin = cache_lookup(cache_dir, key)
        if cubin is not None:
            ret.cubin = cubin
            return ret

    # Compile MLIR module and generate cubin
    with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.bytecode', prefix=func_desc.name,
                                     dir=context.config.temp_dir, delete=False) as f:
        f.write(bytecode_buf)
        f.flush()

        try:
            cubin_file = compile_cubin(f.name, compiler_options, sm_arch,
                                       timeout_sec=context.config.compiler_timeout_sec)
        except TileCompilerError as e:
            if context.config.enable_crash_dump:
                anonymized_bytecode = _get_bytecode(ir_keeper, compiler_options,
                                                    anonymize_debug_info=True)

                _compiler_crash_dump(ir_keeper.final_ir, func_desc.name,
                                     anonymized_bytecode, e.message,
                                     e.compiler_flags, e.compiler_version)

            raise e
        ret.cubin = Path(cubin_file).read_bytes()

    if cache_dir is not None and key is not None:
        cache_store(cache_dir, key, ret.cubin)
        evict_lru(cache_dir, context.config.cache_size_limit)

    return ret


def _tileiras_effective_opt_and_device_debug(
    compiler_options: CompilerOptions, sm_arch: str
) -> tuple[int, bool]:
    """
    When EXPERIMENTAL_CUDA_TILE_DEBUG_BUILD is set, tileiras must use -O0 and
    --device-debug; the disk cache key must match (see cache_key).
    """
    if EXPERIMENTAL_CUDA_TILE_DEBUG_BUILD:
        return 0, True
    return compiler_options.opt_level_for_target(sm_arch), False


def is_windows() -> bool:
    return sys.platform == "win32"


def _get_cuda_home() -> Optional[str]:
    if is_windows():
        if (ret := os.environ.get("CUDA_PATH")):
            return ret
    return os.environ.get("CUDA_HOME")


@dataclass
class _CompilerBinary:
    path: str
    bin_path: str
    ld_path: str
    pass_cuda_home_var: bool

    def run(self,
            args: list[str],
            flags: list[str],
            timeout_sec: float | None = None):
        command = [self.path, *args]

        logger.debug(f"Invoke tile compiler: {' '.join(command + flags)}\n"
                     f"LD_LIBRARY_PATH:{self.ld_path}\n"
                     f"PATH:{self.bin_path}")
        try:
            env = os.environ.copy()
            env['LD_LIBRARY_PATH'] = self.ld_path
            env['PATH'] = self.bin_path
            if not self.pass_cuda_home_var:
                for key in {"CUDA_HOME", "CUDA_PATH"}:
                    env.pop(key, None)
            subprocess.run(command + flags, env=env, check=True, capture_output=True,
                           timeout=timeout_sec)
        except subprocess.CalledProcessError as e:
            raise TileCompilerExecutionError(e.returncode, e.stderr.decode(), ' '.join(flags),
                                             _try_get_compiler_version(self.path))
        except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
            message = (f"`tileiras` compiler exceeded timeout {timeout_sec}s. "
                       "Using a smaller tile size may reduce compilation time.")
            raise TileCompilerTimeoutError(message, ' '.join(flags),
                                           _try_get_compiler_version(self.path))


_PIP_TILEIRAS_PACKAGES = (
    "nvidia-cuda-tileiras",
    "nvidia-cuda-nvcc",
    "nvidia-nvvm",
)


def _get_major_minor(version_str: str) -> tuple[int, int]:
    parts = version_str.split(".")
    return int(parts[0]), int(parts[1])


def _find_pip_tileiras() -> Optional[str]:
    versions: dict[str, str] = {}
    for pkg in _PIP_TILEIRAS_PACKAGES:
        try:
            versions[pkg] = importlib.metadata.version(pkg)
        except importlib.metadata.PackageNotFoundError:
            return None

    majors_minors = {pkg: _get_major_minor(v) for pkg, v in versions.items()}
    unique = set(majors_minors.values())
    if len(unique) != 1:
        details = ", ".join(f"{pkg} {versions[pkg]}" for pkg in _PIP_TILEIRAS_PACKAGES)
        warnings.warn(
            f"Installed NVIDIA pip packages have mismatched versions ({details}). "
            "Falling back to system tileiras.",
            stacklevel=3,
        )
        return None

    try:
        import nvidia.cu13 as cu13_pkg
        cu13_root = cu13_pkg.__path__[0]
    except (ImportError, AttributeError, IndexError):
        logger.debug("Fail to get nvidia.cu13 package path.", exc_info=True)
        return None

    pip_bin_dir = os.path.join(cu13_root, "bin")
    res = shutil.which("tileiras", path=pip_bin_dir)
    if res is None:
        logger.debug("Fail to find tileiras under nvidia.cu13 path.")
        return None

    logger.debug(f"Found tileiras from pip package: {res}")
    return res


@cache
def _find_compiler_bin() -> _CompilerBinary:
    bin_path = os.environ.get('PATH', '')
    ld_path = os.environ.get('LD_LIBRARY_PATH', "") if not is_windows() else ""

    # search from nvidia-cuda-tileiras pip package
    logger.debug("Searching tileiras from nvidia pip package")
    res = _find_pip_tileiras()
    if res is not None:
        return _CompilerBinary(res, bin_path, ld_path, pass_cuda_home_var=False)

    # search under PATH
    logger.debug(f"Searching tileiras: {bin_path}")
    if (res := shutil.which("tileiras")):
        return _CompilerBinary(res, bin_path, ld_path, pass_cuda_home_var=True)

    # search under CUDA_HOME
    if (cuda_home := _get_cuda_home()):
        cuda_bin_path = os.path.join(cuda_home, 'bin')
        logger.debug(f"Searching tileiras: {cuda_bin_path}")
        if (res := shutil.which("tileiras", path=cuda_bin_path)):
            bin_path = bin_path + ":" + cuda_bin_path
            return _CompilerBinary(res, bin_path, ld_path, pass_cuda_home_var=True)

    # Try default CUDA Toolkit installation paths as a fallback
    res = _find_compiler_in_default_cuda_toolkit_paths()
    if res is not None:
        tileiras_path, bin_path = res
        return _CompilerBinary(tileiras_path, bin_path, ld_path, pass_cuda_home_var=False)

    cuda_home_var = "CUDA_PATH" if is_windows() else "CUDA_HOME"
    raise FileNotFoundError("'tileiras' compiler not found, "
                            "make sure it is available as a python package via "
                            "`pip install cuda-tile[tileiras]` or "
                            f"available in $PATH or ${cuda_home_var}/bin via system CTK (13.1+)"
                            " installation.")


_SUPPORTED_VERSIONS = [
    BytecodeVersion.V_13_1,
    BytecodeVersion.V_13_2,
    BytecodeVersion.V_13_3,
]


def _all_bytecode_versions(allow_dev: bool = False) -> Sequence[BytecodeVersion]:
    return BytecodeVersion if allow_dev else _SUPPORTED_VERSIONS


@cache
def _get_max_supported_bytecode_version(temp_dir: str, allow_dev: bool = False) -> BytecodeVersion:
    binary = _find_compiler_bin()
    flags = ["--gpu-name", "sm_120"]
    for version in reversed(_all_bytecode_versions(allow_dev)):
        probe = bytearray()
        with bc.write_bytecode(num_functions=0, buf=probe, version=version):
            pass

        with tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.bytecode', prefix=f"probe{version}",
                                         dir=temp_dir, delete=False) as f_in, \
            tempfile.NamedTemporaryFile(suffix='.cubin', prefix=f"probe{version}",
                                        dir=temp_dir, delete=False) as f_out:
            f_in.write(probe)

        try:
            binary.run([f_in.name, "-o", f_out.name], flags)
        except TileCompilerError:
            continue

        return version

    warnings.warn("Failed to detect the maximum supported TileIR bytecode version;"
                  " falling back to 13.1.")
    return BytecodeVersion.V_13_1


def _find_compiler_in_default_cuda_toolkit_paths() -> tuple[str, str] | None:
    binary_name = "tileiras.exe" if is_windows() else "tileiras"
    for toolkit_path in _get_default_cuda_toolkit_paths():
        bin_path = os.path.join(toolkit_path, "bin")
        p = os.path.join(bin_path, binary_name)
        if os.path.exists(p) and os.access(p, os.X_OK) and not os.path.isdir(p):
            return p, bin_path
    return None


def _get_default_cuda_toolkit_paths() -> list[str]:
    candidates = []

    if os.name == "nt":
        prefix = "C:\\Program Files\\NVIDIA GPU Computing Toolkit\\CUDA"
        regex = re.compile(r"[vV]([0-9]+)(\.[0-9]+)?")
    else:
        prefix = "/usr/local"
        regex = re.compile(r"cuda-([0-9]+)(\.[0-9]+)?")
        candidates.append((math.inf, math.inf, "cuda"))

    for subdir in os.listdir(prefix):
        m = re.fullmatch(regex, subdir)
        if m is None:
            continue
        major = int(m.group(1))
        minor = m.group(2)
        minor = math.inf if minor is None else int(minor[1:])
        candidates.append((major, minor, subdir))

    return [os.path.join(prefix, subdir)
            for _, _, subdir in reversed(sorted(candidates))]


def _try_get_compiler_version(compiler_bin) -> Optional[str]:
    try:
        res = subprocess.run([str(compiler_bin), "--version"],
                             check=True, capture_output=True, text=True)
        return res.stdout
    except Exception:
        return None


@cache
def _get_compiler_version_string() -> str | None:
    binary = _find_compiler_bin()
    version = _try_get_compiler_version(binary.path)
    return version


@cache
def get_sm_arch() -> str:
    major, minor = get_compute_capability()
    return f'sm_{major}{minor}'


def compile_cubin(
        fname_bytecode: str,
        compiler_options: CompilerOptions,
        sm_arch: str,
        timeout_sec: Optional[float]) -> Path:
    binary = _find_compiler_bin()
    fname_cubin = Path(fname_bytecode).with_suffix(".cubin")
    effective_opt, use_device_debug = _tileiras_effective_opt_and_device_debug(
        compiler_options, sm_arch
    )

    args = [str(fname_bytecode), "-o", str(fname_cubin)]

    flags: list[str] = [
        "--gpu-name",
        sm_arch,
        f"-O{effective_opt}",
    ]
    if use_device_debug:
        flags.append("--device-debug")
    else:
        flags.append("--lineinfo")

    binary.run(args, flags, timeout_sec)
    return fname_cubin

82	    if num_worker_warps is not None:
83	        if num_worker_warps not in (4, 8):
84	            raise ValueError(f'num_worker_warps should be either 4 or 8,'
85	                             f' got {num_worker_warps}')
86

1	# SPDX-FileCopyrightText: Copyright (c) <2025> NVIDIA CORPORATION & AFFILIATES. All rights reserved.
2	#
3	# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
4	import dataclasses
5	import linecache
6	import os.path
7	import re
8	from dataclasses import dataclass
9	from typing import Optional
10	from unicodedata import east_asian_width
11	
12	
13	@dataclass(eq=False, frozen=True)
14	class FunctionDesc:
15	    name: str | None  # None for lambdas
16	    filename: str
17	    line: int  # 1-based
18	    column: int  # 1-based
19	    # If this FunctionDesc represents a concrete specialization of a source
20	    # function other than the kernel entry point, this value will hold a
21	    # unique identifier, which is used to distinguish distinct specialized
22	    # functions in debug info.
23	    specialization_id: str | None = None
24	    # True for the FunctionDesc that represents the kernel entry point.
25	    is_entry: bool = False
26	
27	    def __str__(self):
28	        return f"'{self.name}' @{self.filename}:{self.line}:{self.column}"
29	
30	    def short_str(self):
31	        if self.name is None:
32	            base_name = os.path.basename(self.filename)
33	            return f"<lambda at {base_name}:{self.line}:{self.column}>"
34	        else:
35	            return f"<function {self.name}>"
36	
37	
38	@dataclass(slots=True, frozen=True)
39	class Loc:
40	    line: int
41	    col: int
42	    filename: Optional[str] = None
43	    last_line: Optional[int] = None
44	    end_col: Optional[int] = None
45	    function: Optional[FunctionDesc] = None
46	    call_site: Optional["Loc"] = None
47	
48	    def with_call_site(self, call_site) -> "Loc":
49	        return dataclasses.replace(self, call_site=call_site)
50	
51	    def __str__(self) -> str:
52	        if self.filename:
53	            return f"{self.filename}:{self.line}:{self.col}"
54	        return f"<unknown>:{self.line}:{self.col}"
55	
56	    @classmethod
57	    def unknown(cls) -> "Loc":
58	        return _unknown_loc
59	
60	    def is_unknown(self) -> bool:
61	        return self is _unknown_loc
62	
63	    def __enter__(self):
64	        return self
65	
66	    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb):
67	        if exc_type is not None and issubclass(exc_type, TileError):
68	            if exc_val.loc.is_unknown():
69	                exc_val.loc = self
70	
71	
72	_unknown_loc = Loc(line=0, col=0, filename=None)
73	
74	
75	# Returns the visual column width of a string, accounting for double-wide characters
76	def _wcwidth(s: str) -> int:
77	    return sum(2 if east_asian_width(c) in ("W", "F") else 1 for c in s)
78	
79	
80	def format_location(loc: Loc):
81	    frames = []
82	    while loc is not None:
83	        frames.append(loc)
84	        loc = loc.call_site
85	    return "".join(_format_location_frame(x) for x in reversed(frames))
86	
87	
88	def _format_location_frame(loc: Loc) -> str:
89	    if loc.is_unknown():
90	        return "Unknown location"
91	
92	    if loc.last_line is None or loc.last_line == loc.line:
93	        lines_str = f"line {loc.line}"
94	    else:
95	        lines_str = f"lines {loc.line}--{loc.last_line}"
96	
97	    line_text = linecache.getline(loc.filename, loc.line)
98	    if line_text.endswith("\n"):
99	        line_text = line_text[:-1]
100	
101	    line_bytes = line_text.encode()
102	
103	    if loc.end_col is None or loc.last_line is None or loc.last_line != loc.line:
104	        end_col = len(line_bytes)
105	    else:
106	        end_col = loc.end_col
107	
108	    visual_col = _wcwidth(line_bytes[:loc.col].decode())
109	    if end_col == loc.col + 1:
110	        end_visual_col = visual_col + 1
111	        cols_str = f"col {visual_col + 1}"
112	    else:
113	        end_visual_col = _wcwidth(line_bytes[:end_col].decode())
114	        cols_str = f"col {visual_col + 1}-{end_visual_col}"
115	
116	    spaces = " " * visual_col
117	    carets = "^" * (end_visual_col - visual_col)
118	
119	    func_str = "" if loc.function is None else f", in {loc.function.name}"
120	
121	    return (f'  "{loc.filename}", {lines_str}, {cols_str}{func_str}:\n'
122	            f"    {line_text}\n"
123	            f"    {spaces}{carets}\n")
124	
125	
126	class TileError(Exception):
127	    def __init__(self, message: str, loc: Loc = Loc.unknown()):
128	        self.loc = loc
129	        self.message = message
130	
131	    def __str__(self):
132	        return f"{self.message}\n{format_location(self.loc)}"
133	
134	
135	class TileSyntaxError(TileError):
136	    """Exception when a python syntax not supported by cuTile is encountered."""
137	    pass
138	
139	
140	class TileTypeError(TileError):
141	    """Exception when an unexpected type or |data type| is encountered."""
142	    pass
143	
144	
145	class TileRecursionError(TileError):
146	    """Thrown at compile time to indicate that the recursion limit has been reached
147	    when inlining a function call.
148	    """
149	
150	
151	class TileValueError(TileError):
152	    """Exception when an unexpected python value is encountered."""
153	    pass
154	
155	
156	class TileUnsupportedFeatureError(TileError):
157	    """Exception when a feature is not supported by the underlying compiler or
158	      the GPU architecture."""
159	    pass
160	
161	
162	class TileInternalError(TileError):
163	    pass
164	
165	
166	class TileStaticEvalError(TileError):
167	    """Thrown at compile time when the expression inside static_eval() violates the compile-time
168	    evaluation constraints."""
169	
170	
171	class TileStaticAssertionError(TileError):
172	    """Thrown at compile time when the condition of static_assert() evaluates to False."""
173	
174	    def __init__(self, message: str, loc: Loc = Loc.unknown()):
175	        full_message = "Static assertion failed"
176	        if len(message) > 0:
177	            full_message += ": " + message
178	        super().__init__(full_message, loc)
179	
180	
181	class ConstantNotFoundError(Exception):
182	    pass
183	
184	
185	# Simple: loc("file":line:col): error: ...
186	LOC_RE_SIMPLE = re.compile(
187	    r'loc\("([^"]+)"(?::(\d+):(\d+))?\):\s*error:\s*(.*)',
188	    re.I,
189	)
190	
191	# Fused/debug wrapper: loc(fused<...>["file":line:col]): error: ...
192	LOC_RE_FUSED = re.compile(
193	    r'loc\((?:[^)]*?)\["([^"]+)":(\d+):(\d+)\]\):\s*error:\s*(.*)',
194	    re.I,
195	)
196	
197	# error: ...
198	ERROR_RE = re.compile(r'^\s*error:\s*(.*)', re.I)
199	
200	
201	def _parse_tileir_stderr(stderr: str) -> tuple[str, Optional[Loc]]:
202	    msgs = []
203	    loc = None
204	    for line in stderr.splitlines():
205	        msg = None
206	        for loc_re in (LOC_RE_SIMPLE, LOC_RE_FUSED):
207	            if m := loc_re.search(line):
208	                file, line, col, msg = m.groups()
209	                if loc is None:
210	                    # Only capture the first location
211	                    loc = Loc(int(line) if line else None, int(col) if col else None, file)
212	                msg = msg.strip()
213	                break
214	        if msg is None and (m := ERROR_RE.search(line)):
215	            msg = m.group(1).strip()
216	        if msg is None:
217	            # fallback to the original line
218	            msg = line
219	        msgs.append(msg)
220	    return "\n".join(msgs), loc
221	
222	
223	class TileCompilerError(TileInternalError):
224	    def __init__(self,
225	                 message: str,
226	                 loc: Loc,
227	                 compiler_flags: str,
228	                 compiler_version: Optional[str]):
229	        super().__init__(message, loc)
230	        self.compiler_flags = compiler_flags
231	        self.compiler_version = compiler_version
232	
233	
234	class TileCompilerExecutionError(TileCompilerError):
235	    """Exception when ``tileiras`` compiler throws an error."""
236	    def __init__(self,
237	                 return_code: int,
238	                 stderr: str,
239	                 compiler_flags: str,
240	                 compiler_version: Optional[str]):
241	        message, loc = _parse_tileir_stderr(stderr)
242	        if loc is None:
243	            loc = _unknown_loc
244	        super().__init__(f"Return code {return_code}\n{message}", loc,
245	                         compiler_flags, compiler_version)
246	
247	
248	class TileCompilerTimeoutError(TileCompilerError):
249	    """Exception when ``tileiras`` compiler timeout limit is exceeded."""
250	    def __init__(self,
251	                 message: str,
252	                 compiler_flags: str,
253	                 compiler_version: Optional[str]):
254	        super().__init__(message, _unknown_loc, compiler_flags, compiler_version)
255

11	    V_13_3 = 130300
12	
13	    # dev only

15	
16	    def major(self) -> int:
17	        return self._value_ // 10000
18	
19	    def minor(self) -> int:
20	        return (self._value_ // 100) % 100
21	
22	    def tag(self) -> int:
23	        return self._value_ % 100
24	
25	    def as_string(self) -> str:
26	        if self.tag() == 0:
27	            return f"{self.major()}.{self.minor()}"
28	        else:
29	            return f"{self.major()}.{self.minor()}.{self.tag()}"
